_metadata.description: Content representation reference — surfacing whose voices/authors/examples appear in a course's content for HUMAN review. Evidence-based: inventories named sources, does NOT infer demographics. EXPERIMENTAL/UNWIRED v0.1.
_metadata.runtime_strategy_rationale: Body MD ~3,800 chars / 4 ≈ 950 tokens — 'embed' band, but set read_at_runtime per the selective_load pattern shared by the other knowledge files (canvas_course_expert loads a few per task). Moot until wired.

scope: Surfacing whose voices/authors/examples a course's content draws on, so an instructor can judge representation. Checks SOURCE material, not structure. Boundaries: structure/scaffolding lives in cognitive_load + course_design_language; outcome/rubric/syllabus quality have their own files. EXPERIMENTAL/UNWIRED — built ahead of an explicit decision to wire it.

facts.definition: Across a course's readings, cited authors, quoted experts, examples, and case studies — whose perspectives appear, and how concentrated are they? Two structurally identical courses can draw on a narrow or a broad range of voices; representation is about the source material, not the scaffolding.
citations: University of Chicago Chicago Center for Teaching — Inclusive Pedagogy

facts.definition: Representation is a human judgment. The audit inventories the named sources it can detect (citation/attribution patterns) and presents a counted, deduplicated list; it does NOT infer demographic attributes (gender/ethnicity/etc.) of those names — automated demographic inference is error-prone and ethically fraught. The instructor reviews the list and makes the call. Same posture as rubrics_knowledge Criterion 1 and syllabus_knowledge detection.
citations: Tatum, B.D. (1997/2017) Why Are All the Black Kids Sitting Together in the Cafeteria?

facts.term: What It Can and Can't See
facts.definition: Can: inline citation/attribution patterns (Author (Year), 'by ...', 'according to ...', quoted attributions) in page bodies, assignment descriptions, syllabus. Can't (today): authors inside linked PDFs/files, video creators, publisher metadata, sources only named in external links. A course delivering readings as attachments will surface little — a limitation, not a finding. Extraction from arbitrary HTML is heuristic; 'not detected' != 'not present'.

A content-source inventory: distinct named sources detected + mention counts, which content items each appears in, a concentration note (N distinct across M items), and an explicit reminder that this is review data and demographics are not inferred. No pass/fail verdict.

sources: University of Chicago, Chicago Center for Teaching & Integrated Learning. Inclusive Pedagogy (representation of perspectives/scholars).
Tatum, B. D. (1997/2017). Why Are All the Black Kids Sitting Together in the Cafeteria? Basic Books.
General inclusive-curriculum practice ('diversify the syllabus / cite a range of scholars').

validation.status: EXPERIMENTAL / UNWIRED v0.1 — built from the PTC deep-dive (Ch 9; public sources only). Not consumed by canvas_course_expert or course_audit. Sandbox-exercised by content_representation_audit.py; promote + wire only after a real decision that the surface is useful and appropriate.

changes: Created from the PTC full-book deep-dive (new-topic discovery, Ch 9 inclusive-pedagogy thread). Public sources only (UChicago Inclusive Pedagogy, Tatum) — never the internal PTC manuscript. Evidence-based: surface named sources for human review, no demographic inference. EXPERIMENTAL/UNWIRED — grounds content_representation_audit.py; not wired into the agent or course_audit pending an explicit decision.
